stem: A pupil is writing a 'FizzBuzz' program. For every number from 1 to 30 the program prints 'Fizz' if the number is a multiple of 3, 'Buzz' if it is a multiple of 5, 'FizzBuzz' if both, otherwise the number itself.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Accept OCR pseudocode or Python. Look for: loop covers 1..30 inclusive, divisibility tests use MOD/%, the both-multiples case (15) is checked first OR the if/elif ordering produces correct output, prints either Fizz/Buzz/FizzBuzz or n.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the FizzBuzz program for the numbers 1 to 30 inclusive.

mark_points.text: loops over the values 1 to 30 inclusive
tests divisibility using MOD / % (or equivalent) for 3 and 5
handles the multiples-of-both case correctly (test 15 first, OR if/elif ordering)
prints Fizz / Buzz / FizzBuzz / n in the right cases

misconceptions.label: Fizz and Buzz instead of FizzBuzz
misconceptions.description: If 3 is checked before 15, n=15 prints 'Fizz' and exits — losing the FizzBuzz mark. Either combine 3+5 first or use elif ordering.
